{{Quote|Everyone who works for me was born on this planet, and they know that the only way ''any'' of us is going to survive is if we all pull together. We're more than neighbors here—we're a ''community''.|Darial Anglethorn, to Luke Skywalker|<ref name="SW66" />}}
'''Beheboth''' was a desert [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Sanbra sector/Legends|Sanbra sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Beheboth was home to the [[Tirrith]], a unique empathic gaseous culture of hive-minded [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] particles, which thrived even among the planet's inhospitably arid and dry climate. [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]], and later [[Human/Legends|Humans]], settled Beheboth during the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] era, dotting the world with [[Moisture farm/Legends|moisture farms]] and small towns. During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], Beheboth's fledgling economy came under heavy threat from [[brigand]]s who manipulated the Tirrith into aiding them in raids on local moisture farms, a threat ended by [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] hero [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and local leader [[Darial Anglethorn]], who later became Beheboth's first representative to the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|New Republic Senate]].

==Description==
{{Quote|But it hasn't rained here in years!|Darial Anglethorn|<ref name="SW66" />}}
[[File:Garrotine.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The town of Garrotine sits amidst Beheboth's vast deserts.]]
A backwater [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="SW66" /> located along an offshoot of the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]] between [[Arbra/Legends|Arbra]] and [[Golrath/Legends|Golrath]], Beheboth was a planet where [[Water/Legends|water]] was an extremely scarce and valuable resource. Beheboth was the fifth planet<ref name="Beheboth" /> in its [[Beheboth system/Legends|system]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim's]] [[Sanbra sector/Legends|Sanbra sector]],<ref name="AtlasOnline" /> and had three [[Moon/Legends|moons]].<ref name="Beheboth" /> Baking at day and frigid at night, Beheboth was a planet of sweeping, arid plains and imposing rock formations, including large caves and [[Mountain/Legends|mountain]] ranges.<ref name="SW66" /> What little surface water was found on Beheboth came from a few salty natural springs and shallow lakes, and the planet was often wracked by salt and alkaline dust storms.<ref name="Beheboth" />

Although inhospitable to life, Beheboth was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of an extremely unique [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] lifeform known as the [[Tirrith]], an empathic gaseous culture of hive-minded [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] particles that held considerable power when joined together as one.<ref name="SW66" /> The Tirrith had the ability to alter Beheboth's atmosphere, and in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], its seeding of the planet's clouds to cause the planet's first rain in centuries helped bring about a minor return in some hardscrabble plant life—albeit mostly limited to the mountains—and an uptick in animal life.<ref name="Beheboth" /> The planet's deserts teemed with [[Sand louse|sand lice]], which had a habit of spoiling settlers' food.<ref name="SW66" />

==History==
{{Quote|…the brigands have taken everything else from us—water, commerce, sometimes our lives—let's not let them steal the common decency that's all some of us have left!|Darial Anglethorn|<ref name="SW66" />}}
First settled by [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]] during the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] era, Beheboth's original colonists were gradually replaced by [[Human/Legends|Humans]] who dotted the planet's sparse landscape with [[Moisture farm/Legends|moisture farms]], and building a ramshackle economy around subsistence agriculture and [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] and other illegal trades. Although nominally a member of the Republic and later officially annexed by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Beheboth was regarded as a minor world and was allowed little say in [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Politics/Legends|politics]], receiving only [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]-based regional representatives who never visited Beheboth.<ref name="Beheboth" /> In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], a criminal named [[Gideon Longspar]] learned how to manipulate the native Tirrith into aiding him and a gang of [[brigand]]s in ransacking many of the planet's moisture farms, stealing every drop of water and reselling the liquid for exorbitant prices on the [[Black market/Legends|black market]]—a scheme made even more devastating by the fact that, at the time, Beheboth had not seen rain in years.<ref name="SW66" />

[[File:Beheboth_civil_war.jpg|thumb|right|175px|Luke Skywalker and Darial Anglethorn face down an army of brigands.]]
With many locals forced into desperate situations, [[Darial Anglethorn]], the owner of the planet's largest moisture farm, took a leadership role, organizing defense efforts and overseeing the distribution of water to those in need. When the raids were at their height, [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] was forced to land on Beheboth after his [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] had sustained damage in a [[Raid on Golrath Station|raid]] on nearby Golrath. Skywalker soon became acquainted with Anglethorn, who recruited him to aid in her defense efforts against Longspar's brigands. After Anglethorn's farm was raided and Anglethorn herself kidnapped, Skywalker moved on Longspar's mountain stronghold himself, ultimately gaining the aid of the vengeful Tirrith in freeing Anglethorn and defeating Longspar's gang. As thanks, the Tirrith seeded Beheboth's clouds and started a downpour of rain, providing desperately needed water to Beheboth.<ref name="SW66" />

In the aftermath of the water bandits' defeat, Rebel and later [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] troops maintained a presence on the world in order to ensure no further abuse of the Tirrith, which quickly returned to its reclusive ways and ignored multiple entreaties to bring down more rain.<ref name="Gamer 7" /> Despite this, the rains caused a small revitalization in native plant and animal life and led to a period of economic growth, although within a few years a prolonged drought began to threaten these advances. In [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], Anglethorn was appointed Beheboth's first representative to the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|New Republic Senate]], a role she served while continuing to work her own moisture farm and investigate crimes as elected peacekeeper of the town of [[Prosperity (Beheboth)|Prosperity]]. The [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] itself also established a presence on Beheboth, setting up an [[Outpost B-2|outpost]] in Longspar's old mountain fortress.<ref name="Beheboth" />

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Anglethorn discovered yet another attempt to manipulate the Tirrith, this time by disgraced former [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] [[Mils Giel]], who had settled on Beheboth after suffering a crushing [[Raid on Golrath Station|defeat]] on nearby Golrath. After investigating the mysterious death of a former farm worker named [[Berl Matoone]], Anglethorn discovered a connection to Giel—sensing that had stumbled onto something big, she asked for aid from her old friend Skywalker, who was now a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]. Skywalker arrived on Beheboth in time to save Anglethorn's life after an explosive chase with Giel, and uncovered the former military officer's plot to weaponize the Tirrith and use them to restore his status in the Imperial hierarchy. Giel used the Tirrith to kill the New Republic staff at their mountain outpost, but was forced to flee Beheboth after being discovered by Skywalker and Anglethorn.<ref name="Beheboth" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|They're mostly farmers, too. Or at least they were before the ''brigands'' arrived.|Darial Anglethorn|<ref name="SW66" />}}
[[File:Behebothan.jpg|thumb|left|250px|A group of Behebothans accost Luke Skywalker.]]
Beheboth was believed to be devoid of native sentient life until the discovery of the Tirrith in 3 ABY. Although Tirrith hives had previously been sighted by settlers, they had only been perceived as unusual manifestations of chemicals in Beheboth's [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]. However, the Tirrith was in fact a highly intelligent and powerful hive entity with the ability to alter the chemical composition of the planet's atmosphere, although its reclusive and pacifistic nature isolated it from the planetary community at large. Beheboth was considered ill-populated during the era of the Galactic Civil War, but supported a community of sentients aside from the native Tirrith cultures, many of them moisture farmers and their workers.<ref name="SW66" />

Although the population of Beheboth was approximately 17 million, over 99 percent of that was Tirrith—the planet's Human colonists, who replaced the Bothans who originally settled the world, never topped the 60,000-80,000 range in number. Many of the Humans native to Beheboth displayed cosmetic mutations from baseline Humans, including [[Color/Legends|purple]] skin, extra eyebrows or missing fingers, although they were otherwise anatomically identical to the rest of their [[Species/Legends|species]]. Although the cause of these distinctions was unknown by the New Republic era, anthropologists theorized that it was due to the relatively small gene pool, working in tandem with the planet's salt and alkaline dust storms. The raids of Gideon Longspar's brigands put these settlers in mortal danger in 3 ABY, leaving them extremely distrustful and often openly hostile towards perceived outsiders.<ref name="SW66" />

==Locations==
{{Quote|Guess I don't have much choice, now. Either I look up the local trading post and barter for groceries—or I die.|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="SW66" />}}
Although the desert planet Beheboth was often inhospitable to life, several bustling desert towns grew up between the moisture farms that were the lifeblood of Beheboth's economy, including Garrotine.<ref name="SW66" /> These towns were the center of Beheboth's society, as the planet did not have any larger government, with authority localized among the individual town councils. Some of these grew significantly after the Tirrith brought rain to the planet in 3 ABY—Garrotine, renamed "Prosperity," expanded into a rowdy town with over a dozen [[Cantina/Legends|cantinas]] in just five years. However, these towns were still very much ramshackle frontier societies, with very few licensed [[Doctor/Legends|doctors]] working on the planet by 8 ABY.<ref name="Beheboth" />

==Behind the scenes==
Beheboth first appeared as the setting of [[Star Wars (1977) 66|''Star Wars'' (1977) 66]], written by [[David Michelinie]], illustrated by [[Walter Simonson|Walt Simonson]] and released in [[1982]].<ref name="SW66" /> Significant further information on the planet was provided in the [[2003]] [[Wizards of the Coast]] article ''[[Beheboth: Blood and Water]]'', written by [[Cory J. Herndon]].<ref name="Beheboth" />
